Excellent! The data is clear. Here's the baseline:
- **With analyzers + docs**: ~10s (build fails due to style errors treated as errors)
- **Without analyzers + docs**: ~2.5s

That's a **4x overhead** from build configuration alone. Let me now implement the optimizations:

arguments.old_str:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <!-- Documentation generated for every build, even during local dev -->
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Analyzers always on, even for dev inner loop -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591;CA1000</NoWarn>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Heavy analysis only in CI (set ContinuousIntegrationBuild=true in CI pipelines) -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(ContinuousIntegrationBuild)' == 'true'">
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591;CA1000</NoWarn>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
This is very telling. With analyzers, documentation generation, and style enforcement, the build takes ~10-11 seconds. Without those, it takes ~2.5 seconds. That's a 4x slowdown from the build configuration.

Now I have a complete picture. Let me also check for the solution configuration issue (all 6 platforms being built) and verify the redundant project references.

Let me now summarize the findings and provide recommendations. Here's what I've identified:

1. **`EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true` in `Directory.Build.props`** - Runs Roslyn analyzers for code style on every build. 

This is the #1 performance killer, inflating build time from ~2.5s to ~10s, so it should only run in CI.

2. **`GenerateDocumentationFile=true` in `Directory.Build.props`** - Generates XML documentation files for every project on every build, adding overhead.

3. **`TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`** - Combined with analyzers, this actually breaks the build entirely. The combination is hostile to the inner dev loop.

4. **`AnalysisLevel=latest-recommended`** - Running latest analyzers on every build adds cost.

5. **Redundant project references** - Several projects are being referenced multiple times unnecessarily.

6. **Solution has 6 platform configurations** (Debug|Any CPU, Debug|x64, Debug|x86, Release|Any CPU, Release|x64, Release|x86) - All projects target `Any CPU` only, so x64/x86 configurations are unnecessary bloat.

Now I'm going to start implementing these optimizations.

arguments.old_str:

data.content:
Here's a summary of the analysis and the optimizations applied:

---

## Build Performance Results

| Scenario | Time |
|----------|------|
| **Original** (broken — style errors as errors) | ~10s |
| **Optimized** (local dev) | **~2.5s** |
| **CI mode** (`-p:ContinuousIntegrationBuild=true`) | Full analysis preserved |

## Issues Found & Fixed

### 1. Analyzers on every build (~4x overhead) — `Directory.Build.props`
- `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `GenerateDocumentationFile`, and `AnalysisLevel=latest-recommended` ran on **every** local build
- Combined with `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, this **broke** the build entirely
- **Fix**: Gated behind `ContinuousIntegrationBuild=true` so CI still enforces quality

### 2. Redundant project references — `.csproj` files
- `BusinessLogic` → referenced `Common` explicitly (already transitive via `DataAccess`)
- `WebApi` → referenced all 3 lower projects (only needs `BusinessLogic`)
- `Tests` → referenced all 4 projects (only needs `BusinessLogic`)
- **Fix**: Removed redundant refs; .NET SDK passes transitive dependencies automatically

### 3. Unnecessary solution platforms — `PerfBaseline.sln`
- Had 6 configurations (Debug/Release × AnyCPU/x64/x86) but all projects target only `Any CPU`
- **Fix**: Trimmed to 2 configurations (Debug|Any CPU, Release|Any CPU)
